description: Professional OS ecosystem - proof-based career platform

do: Frame exploration as discovery journeys with phases and evolution
Solve cold start problems with Trojan Horse entry strategies
Segment audiences with distinct value propositions and entry points
Provide three-tier candidate profiles (conventional/adjacent/wild card)
Generate downloadable PDFs with multiple style variants (Classic/Modern/Corporate)
Use typography variants: Lora+Source Sans (classic), Inter (modern), Work Sans (corporate)
Implement personalized file naming: JustinHarmon_<Variant>.pdf format
Create screen-ready layouts with embedded fonts and elevated contrast
Parse JSON resume data and select/trim content for different audience contexts
Use PromptGen workflow: Parse → Select → Render → Validate → Export
Implement layout strategies: single-column (classic), two-column sidebar (modern), banner header (corporate)
Never revert to previous versions once user approves styling decisions
Use data-adaptive layout modes for sparse vs dense profiles
Design semantic CSS mapping from data fields to visual hierarchy
Implement conditional rendering based on content availability
Create zero-configuration export workflows (30 seconds or less)
Centralize customizable values in CSS variables for easy theming
Optimize web documents for print with explicit page and media rules
Use comprehensive font fallback chains for cross-platform consistency
Diagnose systematically before implementing solutions (two-stage debugging)
Anchor reasoning only in validated product data when positioning
Use proof-of-work credibility layer framing to differentiate from portfolio builders
Position content along awareness → trust → validation pipeline
Follow JSDoc Protocol for all code documentation in Windsurf
Use /** */ comment blocks with required tags for all public functions
Document all parameters with types and return values
Include examples for non-trivial functions and complex implementations
Enforce TypeScript-compatible type syntax in JSDoc annotations
dont: Over-promise on market sizing without validation
Build features before solving cold start problems
Recommend platform switching without migration bridges
framing: Position new products as enhancements before replacements
Show evolution from curiosity → frameworks → market-ready solutions

lexicon.definition: Professional development ecosystem and 'professional OS' for capturing/framing work experiences; alternative to LinkedIn based on proof over performance theater
examples: Core platform: professional identity with proof-based profiles
The Forge: experience engine and agency arm
Career/College Planning: trajectory mapping tool
Hiring Manager Intelligence: team building and candidate discovery

lexicon.definition: Mechanism where skills and experiences have half-lives requiring continuous validation; contrast to static credentials
examples: Skills lose value over time without fresh proof
Professional physics vs LinkedIn's chemistry model

lexicon.definition: Low-friction entry strategy: tool analyzes existing LinkedIn profiles, suggests improvements, publishes back to LinkedIn while gradually pulling users into superior editing experience
examples: Enhancement positioning rather than replacement
Behavioral change over time through superior UX

lexicon.definition: Target segment: risk-averse job seekers who want better LinkedIn outcomes without platform switching
examples: Entry via LinkedIn audit tool
Gradual migration through profile enhancement workflow

lexicon.definition: Target segment: tool-obsessed early adopters (designers, indie makers) who signal status through platform choice
examples: Design-focused users seeking Read.cv/Bento.me alternatives
Early adopters who showcase tools as personal brand

lexicon.definition: Hiring framework providing HR with conventional fit, adjacent transferable, and wild card high-ceiling candidate profiles
examples: Works with existing ATS systems
Expands candidate pool beyond keyword matches

lexicon.definition: Measure of how much descriptive text exists per experience/proof entry; drives PDF layout mode selection
examples: Sparse profiles (minimal descriptions) use MINIMAL mode with larger typography
Dense profiles (detailed descriptions) use STANDARD mode with compact spacing

lexicon.definition: Direct translation from JSON data fields to PDF visual elements without user configuration
examples: title → H2 heading, organization → metadata, description → body text
Eliminates export customization decisions

lexicon.definition: Automatic PDF formatting preset selected based on profile content characteristics
examples: MINIMAL: <100 chars total description, 18pt titles, generous spacing
STANDARD: >100 chars, 14pt titles, compact spacing

lexicon.definition: Experience/proof with no description field or very brief description requiring visual adaptation
examples: Title and organization only, no bullet points
System increases title size to compensate for missing content

lexicon.definition: Different font combinations optimized for specific resume styles and target audiences
examples: Classic: Lora headers + Source Sans body (traditional, readable)
Modern: Inter headers + Inter body (clean, contemporary)
Corporate: Work Sans headers + Work Sans body (professional, streamlined)

lexicon.definition: PDF formatting optimized for digital viewing with embedded fonts and elevated contrast
examples: RGB color profile for screen display
Embedded fonts to prevent rendering issues
Elevated contrast for readability across devices

lexicon.definition: Single JSON input generating multiple styled PDF outputs for different contexts
examples: Classic Edition: traditional single-column with section dividers
Modern Creative: two-column with left sidebar and blue accents
Corporate Streamlined: banner header with right-column highlights

lexicon.definition: Consistent naming convention that includes person name and variant type
examples: JustinHarmon_Classic.pdf
JustinHarmon_Modern.pdf
JustinHarmon_Corporate.pdf

lexicon.definition: Joju's system that translates raw project data into career capital and professional narrative
examples: Joju acts as your personal credibility engine
Proof-of-work credibility layer differentiates from simple portfolio builders
Credibility before visibility as positioning mantra

lexicon.definition: Career validation through demonstrated deliverables rather than traditional credentials
examples: GitHub contributions over computer science degree
Portfolio of shipped products over years of experience
Client testimonials over job titles

lexicon.definition: Joju component that scores and classifies achievements using structured analysis with tier classification and framework alignment heuristics
examples: Rev 01 JSON schema for achievement validation
Scoring system for achievement impact and relevance
Tier classification for achievement hierarchy

lexicon.definition: Joju's positioning as AI-powered version control system for human achievement and identity across platforms and contexts
examples: Trusted, AI-powered version control system for evolving identities
Deep workflow integration with professional tools
Expansion from job search into holistic identity and growth analytics

lexicon.definition: Conversational AI system that structures data while enabling natural storytelling during profile creation
examples: Human-first data experience starting with conversation
AI guides and structures inputs while keeping users in control
Procedural Q&A to reduce onboarding friction

lexicon.definition: Live, trusted data sync system that maintains quality with inline cleaning and visible system activity
examples: Sync from source tools with background activity visibility
Live integrations as streams, not static imports
Privacy-first cleaning and classification with history tracking

lexicon.definition: Joju's core positioning from Foundation Sprint - unifies achievements, narrative, and identity into a living source of truth
examples: Interactive system that responds to user needs and context
Intelligent processing of achievements and career data
Proof ecosystem that validates and connects professional accomplishments
Living source of truth that evolves with user's career journey

lexicon.definition: Joju's code documentation standard using JSDoc format with specific rules for Windsurf development
examples: /** */ comment blocks with brief description ending with period
@param {Type} paramName - Description with type annotations
@returns {Type} Description of return value
@example usage demonstrations for complex functions
@throws {ErrorType} documentation for error conditions
TypeScript-compatible type syntax: {string|number}, {Array<Type>}, {Promise<Type>}

lexicon.definition: Comprehensive template and rules for generating JSDoc comments for JavaScript/TypeScript code in Joju development
examples: Structure: Start /** end */, brief description (third-person singular, period-terminated)
Document ALL parameters with @param {Type} name - Description.
Document return values with @returns {Type} Description.
Type syntax: Primitives {string}, {number}, {boolean}; Complex {Object}, {Array<Type>}, {Promise<Type>}
Optional parameters: [paramName] or {Type|undefined}; Unions: {Type1|Type2}
Rules: Document every exported function/class/module, use TypeScript-compatible types
Include practical examples, mark private code with @private
Add @deprecated with alternatives when applicable, consistent punctuation

agent_references.note: Agent capabilities moved to projects/agent_registry.json to prevent duplication
agent_references.reconstruction: Use 'recreate WCB for <agent_id>' to regenerate from registry

joju_achievement_rev01.description: JSON schema for Joju achievement analysis and validation (Rev 01)

checklists.name: Cold Start Problem Solving
items: Identify Trojan Horse entry strategy (enhancement before replacement)
Design low-friction first-touch experience
Map behavioral change pathway
Segment audiences with distinct entry points
Create migration bridges from existing platforms

checklists.name: PDF Export Quality Validation
items: Phase 1: Data integrity - all JSON fields map correctly to PDF sections
Phase 2: Layout adaptation - sparse profiles use MINIMAL mode, dense use STANDARD
Phase 3: Typography hierarchy - titles 18pt/14pt, metadata 12pt, body 11pt, proper line heights
Phase 4: Print optimization - page breaks clean, margins consistent, fonts embed correctly
Phase 5: Zero config test - user clicks export, gets professional PDF in 30 seconds, no decisions needed

summary_log.topic: PDF export system implementation and typography variants
summary_log.notes: Successfully implemented multi-variant PDF generation system with three distinct styles (Classic, Modern, Corporate). Each variant uses different typography systems and layouts optimized for specific audiences. Added personalized file naming convention and screen-ready formatting with embedded fonts.

summary_log.topic: Resume rendering workflow extraction from ChatGPT session
summary_log.notes: Extracted comprehensive resume rendering patterns from Chat_Mining_Complete report. Added PromptGen workflow (Parse → Select → Render → Validate → Export), layout strategies for each variant, and never-revert versioning policy. Enhanced typography variants with specific font fallbacks and screen optimization techniques.

summary_log.topic: PromptGen 1.0 LLM onboarding system for joju
summary_log.notes: Created comprehensive 4000+ word prompt using conversation_search to reconstruct joju's full ecosystem vision. Documented four interconnected platforms with proof decay, LinkedIn audit Trojan Horse strategy, and three-segment GTM approach.

summary_log.topic: Team science and AI-powered hiring intelligence discovery journey
summary_log.notes: Exploration progressed through 8 phases from curiosity about team building psychology to market-ready 'Three-Profile Blueprint' concept. Journey: team psychology foundations → AI personality analysis → LinkedIn API limitations → diversity challenges in ATS systems → insight that traditional ATS creates homogeneous pools through keyword screening. Culminated in innovation that works with existing HR processes: provides hiring managers three candidate search profiles (conventional fits, adjacent transferable, wild card high-ceiling).

summary_log.topic: Hiring Manager Intelligence tool ideation and refinement
summary_log.notes: Deep product development for tool that translates team dynamics into actionable HR search criteria. Core innovation: empowers hiring managers directly, bypassing HR adoption challenges. Analyzes existing team using Big Five and Belbin frameworks, generates three-profile blueprint for searches. Advanced features discussed: real-time team chemistry modeling, dynamic context awareness, competitive intelligence layers, Team Simulation Mode for test-driving candidate fit.

summary_log.topic: Joju strategic product vision and professional infrastructure positioning
summary_log.notes: Extensive discussion positioning joju as comprehensive professional development infrastructure vs just another profile platform. Multi-faceted vision: core profile functionality, The Forge (project-based experience engine), career planning, hiring manager intelligence. Breakthrough concepts: time-based proof decay (credentials lose value without fresh validation), cross-pollination intelligence between components, positioning as infrastructure other platforms could integrate via APIs.

summary_log.topic: Joju cold start problem and LinkedIn audit Trojan Horse strategy
summary_log.notes: Developed 'LinkedIn audit' Trojan Horse strategy to solve cold start. Three key segments: subtle converts, evangelists, recruiters with distinct entry points and value props.

summary_log.topic: Portfolio JSON to PDF export system (joju export experiment)
summary_log.notes: Built proof-of-concept PDF export feature for joju profiles. Core innovation: data-adaptive layout (MINIMAL mode for sparse profiles, STANDARD for detailed). Automatic semantic field mapping (JSON → visual hierarchy) with zero user configuration. Tested with Matthew Galley resume - proved system handles sparse entries gracefully. 30-second export workflow, browser-based, professional typography (Source Sans Pro/Crimson Text). Strategic fit: supports LinkedIn audit Trojan Horse by providing polished PDF that stays current with joju profile updates. Validates export as key retention feature.

summary_log.topic: GTM strategy and persona mapping (external LLM mining report)
summary_log.notes: Extracted positioning framework from external mining report. Added 'credibility engine' framing to differentiate from portfolio builders. Created two new agent recipes: Persona Matrix Builder (mines target user personas with behaviors and pain points) and Validation Loop Agent (tracks early user feedback and pricing signals). Identified four GTM validation open loops: persona behavior mapping, competitor monitoring, early adopter validation, and scaling metrics. Emphasizes awareness → trust → validation pipeline for content positioning.

Extracted from mining report: Defined Joju Achievement Analysis WCB using PromptGen 1.0 framework. Added full JSON schema for achievements (Rev 01). Established scoring, tier classification, and framework alignment heuristics. Schema and WCB content needs integration into joju system.

summary_log.topic: Foundation Sprint - core purpose and differentiation
summary_log.notes: Completed Foundation Sprint establishing Joju's core purpose, differentiation, and decision criteria through structured workshops. Key outcome: framed Joju as 'interactive, intelligent proof ecosystem' that unifies achievements, narrative, and identity into a living source of truth. Foundation Sprint provided strategic framework that informed subsequent Design Sprint 1.0 tactical implementation.

Completed comprehensive design sprint defining Joju as human-first, AI-augmented 'Career OS.' Core philosophy: human-first data experience with conversational AI intake, live trusted Proof Layer with visible sync activity, playful but precise UX borrowing game mechanics. Long-term vision: AI-powered version control system for human achievement and identity across platforms. Key differentiator: living loop between expression, validation, and iteration. Identified 5 concrete opportunities: AI Intake Partner, live sync architecture, multi-version CV control, radial tagging with game tooltips, inline cleaning with privacy-first controls. Next steps: prototype 3 intake flows, build visible intelligence activity panel, validate recruiter acceptance, establish public feedback channel. Focus on living loop between expression, validation, and iteration. Make Joju feel 'alive' - adaptive, transparent, and empowering. Follow JSDoc Protocol for all code documentation in Windsurf. Use /** */ comment blocks with required tags for all public functions. Document all parameters with types and return values. Include examples for non-trivial functions and complex implementations. Enforce TypeScript-compatible type syntax in JSDoc annotations.

Established JSDoc Protocol for Windsurf Rules as core build philosophy for Joju development. Key requirements: /** */ comment blocks for all public functions, required @param and @returns tags with TypeScript-compatible types, @example for complex functions, @throws for error conditions. Enforcement rules: all exported functions/classes must have JSDoc, all parameters must be documented with types, include examples for non-trivial functions. Protocol ensures consistent, maintainable codebase with clear documentation standards.

Extended JSDoc Protocol with comprehensive generation guide for JavaScript/TypeScript code. Template includes structure requirements (/** */ blocks, third-person descriptions), complete type syntax (primitives, complex types, optionals, unions), and enforcement rules (document every exported function/class/module, TypeScript-compatible types, practical examples). Guide ensures consistent documentation generation across all Joju development with specific formatting and punctuation standards.
